Private Owner Briefing

Prepared for Mat Ishbia

Issue 05 / Game 03 Postgame / July 14, 2026

Suns Summer League Pulse

Final / Phoenix improves to 2-1

Phoenix 95
-
Milwaukee 88

Maluach stays steady. Brea breaks through. Phoenix got meaningful progress from three young players in Game 3.

The Read / AVC Interpretation

Phoenix got progress from three young players.

Maluach posted a third straight double-double and four blocks. Brea followed an 0-for-11 start from three over the first two games by making six against Milwaukee. Peat scored 19. The 95-88 win mattered, but the wider development picture mattered more.

Final / Phoenix

95-88
Phoenix 2-1
19Q1
26Q2
24Q3
26Q4
At halftime
45-44
3-pointers
15-34
Rebounds
42-30
Assists / TO
22 / 16
Khaman Maluach4 blocks / 6-for-6 at the line / 1 turnover
21 PTS / 10 REB
Koby Brea6-for-11 from three / 10 fourth-quarter points
19 PTS
Koa Peat8-for-15 from the field / 6 rebounds / 2 steals
19 PTS
Darius Brown4-for-5 from three / team-high 5 assists
14 PTS

Confirmed

Could Phoenix find perimeter support?

Yes. Brea, Brown and Fleming combined for 13 of Phoenix's 15 made threes.

First half

Phoenix avoided another deep early hole. The Suns trailed by three after one quarter and led by one at halftime.

Rebounding

Phoenix controlled the glass. The Suns won the rebound margin by 12 and the second-chance margin 17-9.

Closing

The Suns responded after Milwaukee took an 87-84 lead. Phoenix finished the final 2:40 on an 11-1 run.

Ball security

Phoenix committed 16 team turnovers. Milwaukee produced 11 steals and scored 19 points off those mistakes.

Transition

Milwaukee won the fast-break margin 16-5. Phoenix's missed chances and turnovers gave the Bucks easier offense.

Game 04 / Wednesday, July 153:00 PM Phoenix

Phoenix vs. Detroit

Thomas & Mack Center, Las Vegas / Arizona's Family Sports and ESPNU.

Owner checkpoint

Can Phoenix carry the same ball movement into a cleaner game against Detroit?

Control

Can Phoenix build and hold a lead?

Milwaukee erased a nine-point deficit and moved ahead with 2:40 left before the Suns recovered.

Finals through July 13 7 games
  • NYK 86 — DET 75↗

    New York earned its first Vegas win.
  • TOR 94 — IND 93↗

    Monday's closest finish was decided by one.
  • ATL 102 — BOS 90↗

    Atlanta was one of two Monday teams to reach 100.
  • DAL 96 — MEM 88↗

    Dallas finished eight points clear of Memphis.
  • CLE 90 — MIA 73↗

    Cleveland held Miami to 73 points.
  • UTA 80 — CHI 63↗

    Chicago posted Monday's lowest total.
  • POR 111 — MIN 84↗

    Portland posted Monday's highest score and widest margin.

  • NBA official game page: Phoenix at Milwaukee↗
  • NBA official scorer's report: Phoenix at Milwaukee↗
  • NBA official Game 1 scorer's report↗
  • NBA official Game 2 scorer's report↗
  • NBA official game page: Phoenix vs. Detroit↗
  • NBA official 2026 Summer League schedule↗
  • ESPN July 13 scoreboard cross-check↗

Verified July 14, 2026, after all eight Monday games. The NBA official scorer's report controls all Game 3 player and team statistics. Brea's 0-for-11 start is derived from the official Game 1 and Game 2 reports; a local recap lists 0-for-10, so that figure is not used. ESPN was used only to cross-check Monday's non-Phoenix finals. AVC Interpretation is analysis based on public information. No direct postgame quotes were included because no official recording or first-party transcript was located by the verification cutoff.

Bottom Line

Detroit will show whether this was one hot shooting night or the start of a more balanced offense.

Prepared exclusively for Mat Ishbia. Public information. Owner-level clarity.

Accuracy over volume. If a fact cannot be verified, omit it.
